page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture compute-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache, with hardware support for AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AES cryptography. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates moderate single-core capabilities but exhibits performance bottlenecks under multi-threaded workloads. Single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression register in the average tier, while multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the weak category. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with read-only large block memory bandwidth measured at 31.76 GB/sec and small block cached read bandwidth at 211.64 GB/sec, both ranking as weak. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor, though the instance maintains average performance for static web serving at 6.28 GB/sec and small-model LLM inference prompt processing at 404.47 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c7.xlarge offers dedicated CPU allocation for compute-focused tasks without the overhead of bundled storage or GPU hardware. However, the low memory-to-CPU ratio and weak multi-core scaling present a tradeoff for memory-intensive or highly parallelized applications. The server is best aligned with workloads such as lightweight single-threaded processing, basic static web serving, and small-scale language model inference, rather than large databases or heavy multi-threaded computing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU performance and static web serving throughput of 6.28 GB/sec, alongside average prompt processing speeds of 404.47 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models. However, the instance exhibits weak multi-core scaling, weak memory bandwidth, and poor multi-threaded decompression. Qualitatively, it provides a dedicated compute profile for workloads that do not require high memory density or local storage. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded processing, static web serving, and small-scale inference tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, showing average single-core but weak multi-core performance.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 compute instance with a one-to-two core-to-memory ratio, optimized for lightweight single-threaded tasks and static web serving.

3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero bundled local storage
Average single-core performance and static web serving throughput
Weak multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth under heavy loads
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and small-scale LLM inference
